zh.lifeSummary: John de Walton是Sir John de Walton和Margaret de Sutton的兒子，出生在英國亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓。他於大約1378年與Elizabeth Butler在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1401年在英國艾可維文，埋葬在英國艾可維文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John de Walton是Sir John de Walton和Margaret de Sutton的儿子，出生在英国亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓。他于大约1378年与Elizabeth Butler在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1401年在英国艾可維文，埋葬在英国艾可維文。

nameMeanings.name: Walton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Walton, which are named with Old English walh ‘foreigner, Briton, serf’ (genitive plural wala), wald ‘woodland’, wall ‘wall’, or wælla ‘well, spring, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

History: George Walton (1741–1804) signed the Declaration of Independence. He was born in Prince Edward County, VA, whither his grandfather had emigrated from England in 1682. He moved to Savannah, GA, and became governor of GA and a prominent jurist.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: John de Walton was born in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England as the son of Sir John de Walton and Margaret de Sutton. He married Elizabeth Butler about 1378, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1401, in Wivenhoe, Essex, England, and was buried in Wivenhoe, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: John de Walton est né à Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, fils de Sir John de Walton et Margaret de Sutton. Il a épousé Elizabeth Butler environ 1378, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1401, à Wivenhoe, Essex, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Wivenhoe, Essex, Angleterre.

Lui sposato Elizabeth Butler circa 1378, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1401, nel Wivenhoe, Essex, England, e fu sepolto a Wivenhoe, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John de WaltonはイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアハンティングドンでSir John de WaltonとMargaret de Suttonの息子として生まれた。約1378年にイギリスイギリスでElizabeth Butlerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1401年にイギリスエセックスウィベンhオで死去し，イギリスエセックスウィベンhオにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Walton(은)는 영국 헌틴그도실르 헌틴그던에서 Sir John de Walton(와)과 Margaret de Sutton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1378년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elizabeth Butler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1401년 영국 에세스 위븐호에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 위븐호에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: John de Walton родился в местности хантингдон, хантингдоншир, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir John de Walton и Margaret de Sutton. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Butler около 1378 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1401 г. в местности вивено, эссекс, Англия и был похоронен в местности вивено, эссекс, Англия.
